title: Discover Your Wellness Plan
subtitle: Answer a few quick questions and we'll provide personalised tips to help you manage stress and improve your wellbeing.

options.label: Brain fog and lack of clarity

questions.question: How often do you experience this?

options.label: Student — I need to study and retain information
Working professional — Long hours, high demands
Active lifestyle — I exercise and want mental edge
Over 50 — I want to maintain cognitive health

questions.question: Which wellness habits do you currently practise?

options.label: I'm looking to start healthier habits

questions.question: What would make the biggest difference in your daily life?

options.label: Feeling calmer and more in control
Bouncing back from tough days faster
Having more balanced energy throughout the day

brain_fog.title: Your Plan to Beat Brain Fog
brain_fog.description: Brain fog can be frustrating, but the good news is that small lifestyle changes can make a big difference. Here are evidence-based strategies tailored to your situation.
tips: Stay hydrated — dehydration is one of the most common causes of mental cloudiness. Aim for 2 litres of water daily.
Take regular screen breaks — follow the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
Prioritise quality sleep — maintain a consistent bedtime and avoid screens for 30 minutes before bed.
Move your body — even a 10-minute walk can increase blood flow to the brain and clear mental haze.
Eat brain-friendly foods — omega-3 rich foods like salmon, walnuts, and leafy greens support cognitive function.

stress.title: Your Personalised Stress Management Plan
stress.description: Stress is your body's natural response to challenges, but chronic stress can take a toll on your wellbeing. These practical strategies can help you regain control.
tips: Practise box breathing — inhale for 4 counts, hold for 4, exhale for 4, hold for 4. Repeat 4 times when you feel overwhelmed.
Schedule daily decompression time — even 15 minutes of an enjoyable activity (reading, walking, music) helps reset your nervous system.
Limit caffeine after midday — caffeine can amplify anxiety and disrupt the sleep you need to recover from stress.
Try progressive muscle relaxation — tense and release each muscle group from toes to head before bed.
Connect with others — social support is one of the strongest buffers against chronic stress. Reach out to a friend or loved one regularly.

memory.title: Tips to Strengthen Your Memory
memory.description: Memory is like a muscle — it gets stronger with the right habits. These science-backed techniques can help you retain and recall information more effectively.
tips: Use spaced repetition — review new information at increasing intervals (1 day, 3 days, 7 days) to move it into long-term memory.
Get 7-9 hours of quality sleep — your brain consolidates memories during deep sleep, so good rest is essential.
Stay physically active — regular aerobic exercise increases the size of the hippocampus, the brain area involved in memory.
Reduce multitasking — focus on one thing at a time to improve encoding. It takes about 8 seconds of focused attention to commit something to memory.
Eat a Mediterranean-style diet — rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and healthy fats, this diet is linked to slower cognitive decline.

focus.title: Your Guide to Better Focus
focus.description: In a world full of distractions, maintaining focus is a skill that can be trained. Here are proven strategies to sharpen your concentration.
tips: Try the Pomodoro Technique — work in focused 25-minute blocks, then take a 5-minute break. After 4 blocks, take a longer 15-30 minute break.
Create a distraction-free zone — silence notifications, use website blockers, and keep your workspace tidy during focused work.
Practise mindfulness meditation — even 10 minutes daily trains your brain to return attention to the present moment.
Exercise regularly — physical activity boosts dopamine and norepinephrine, both critical for sustained attention.
Plan your hardest tasks for your peak energy hours — most people focus best in the morning. Identify your personal peak and protect that time.

energy.title: Your Plan to Boost Mental Energy
energy.description: Mental fatigue often stems from lifestyle factors that can be adjusted. These strategies will help you feel more alert and energised throughout the day.
tips: Optimise your sleep schedule — go to bed and wake up at the same time every day, even on weekends, to regulate your circadian rhythm.
Eat balanced meals — combine protein, complex carbohydrates, and healthy fats to avoid blood sugar crashes that drain mental energy.
Take movement breaks — stand up and move for 5 minutes every hour. Short walks are especially effective at combating afternoon slumps.
Stay hydrated — even mild dehydration (1-2%) can significantly impair energy levels and cognitive performance.
Limit sugar-heavy snacks — they cause rapid energy spikes followed by crashes. Choose nuts, fruit, or yoghurt instead.
